taxonomy: Vesiculina setacea is classified as a species of green algae in the phylum Chlorophyta and the family Dasycladales. It belongs to the genus Vesiculina, which is a group of marine algae characterized by their unique, vesicular (bladder-like) thalli. The specific classification of Vesiculina setacea is Vesiculina setacea (Mayfield) J.T. Harper, 1994. Vesiculina setacea is a marine, benthic species typically found in tropical and subtropical waters. This species is often found growing in dense clusters on rocky substrates.
